inputs.name: accessibleDescription
inputs.description: Defines the accessible description of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleDescriptionRef
inputs.description: Defines the IDs of the elements that describe the component.

inputs.name: accessibleName
inputs.description: Defines the accessible name of the component.

inputs.name: accessibleNameRef
inputs.description: Defines the IDs of the elements that label the component.

inputs.name: headerText
inputs.description:
Defines the component header text.

*Note:** If the `header` slot is set, this property is ignored.

inputs.name: noDataText
inputs.description: Defines the text that is displayed when the component contains no items.

inputs.name: selectionMode
inputs.description:
Defines the selection mode of the component. Since the tree uses a `ui5-list` to display its structure,
the tree modes are exactly the same as the list modes, and are all applicable.

outputs.name: ui5ItemClick
outputs.description: Fired when a tree item is activated.

outputs.name: ui5ItemDelete
outputs.description:
Fired when the Delete button of any tree item is pressed.

*Note:** A Delete button is displayed on each item,
when the component `selectionMode` property is set to `Delete`.

outputs.name: ui5ItemMouseout
outputs.description: Fired when the mouse cursor leaves the tree item borders.

outputs.name: ui5ItemMouseover
outputs.description: Fired when the mouse cursor enters the tree item borders.

outputs.name: ui5ItemToggle
outputs.description:
Fired when a tree item is expanded or collapsed.

*Note:** You can call `preventDefault()` on the event object to suppress the event, if needed.
This may be handy for example if you want to dynamically load tree items upon the user expanding a node.
Even if you prevented the event's default behavior, you can always manually call `toggle()` on a tree item.

outputs.name: ui5Move
outputs.description:
Fired when a movable tree item is moved over a potential drop target during a drag-and-drop operation.

If the new position is valid, prevent the default action of the event using `preventDefault()`.

outputs.name: ui5MoveOver
outputs.description:
Fired when a movable tree item is dropped onto a drop target.

*Note:** The `move` event is fired only if there was a preceding `move-over` event with prevented default action.

outputs.name: ui5SelectionChange
outputs.description:
Fired when selection is changed by user interaction
in `Single`, `SingleStart`, `SingleEnd` and `Multiple` modes.
